action.content:
Correctly close a Dyck-n word.

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: [ { [
A: ] } ]

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < > ( ( [ [ ( { } ) [ < > ] ]
A: ] ) )

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < [ < [ { < [ ] < { } > > } ] > { { ( ) } { < [ < > ] > }
A: } ] >

Q: Complete the rest of the sequence, making sure that the parentheses are closed properly. Input: < { < ( )
A:
action.label: context

action.content:
To correctly close the Dyck-n word, we need to match each opening bracket with a closing bracket of the same type, in the reverse order of their appearance. 

For the input `< { < ( )`, we have the following sequence:
- `<` (opening angle bracket)
- `{` (opening curly brace)
- `<` (opening angle bracket)
- `(` (opening parenthesis)
- `)` (closing parenthesis)

To close this sequence properly, we need to match the closing parenthesis `)` with the opening parenthesis `(`, then the opening angle bracket `<` with the closing angle bracket `>`, and finally the opening curly brace `{` with the closing curly brace `}`.

The correct completion is: `< { < ( ) } > } `
action.label: response
